etymology.description: The word 主任(主任) (zhǔrèn, "director; person in charge") consists of: 主(主) (zhǔ, "master; main") and 任(任) (rèn, "duty; appoint"). 主(主) suggests the concept of a leader or primary entity, while 任(任) refers to a role or responsibility. Together, they form the idea of someone with the primary responsibilities or duties, thus a "director" or "person in charge."

examples.chinese: 他是这个项目的主任(他是這個項目的主任)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì zhège xiàngmù de zhǔrèn.
examples.english: He is the director of this project.

examples.chinese: 学校主任正在开会(學校主任正在開會)。
examples.english: The school director is having a meeting.

examples.chinese: 如果你有问题请找主任(如果你有問題請找主任)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ yǒu wèntí qǐng zhǎo zhǔrèn.
examples.english: If you have any questions, please contact the director.
